evals.prompt: Get this branch merged. There are local changes, no PR yet, and I want you to keep going unless you hit a real blocker.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the wrapper, publishes the branch through a draft PR, updates reviewer-facing PR text, gates review-ready state on verification, then drives review and merge readiness until merged or blocked.
expectations: The response starts from discovered repo and branch state instead of asking broad scope questions.
The agent uses yeet only for the publish/create-PR stage.
The agent marks the PR ready only after local readiness gates and PR body evidence are current.
The agent stops rather than merging if required checks, approvals, ownership, or branch protection block merge.

evals.prompt: PR #42 is already open as a draft. Mark it review-ready and drive it to merge.
evals.expected_output: The agent resumes at the draft/ready gate, refreshes the PR body and state, marks ready when evidence supports it, then handles reviews and merge gates.
expectations: The agent does not create a duplicate PR or replay completed publish work.
The agent uses writing-reviewable-pr-descriptions before publishing readiness claims.
The agent uses pr-review-orchestration for review-ready and merge-readiness decisions.
The agent reports the next blocker if merge cannot proceed.

evals.prompt: Address the unresolved review comments on this PR, rerun the review loop, and merge once it is clean.
evals.expected_output: The agent fetches thread-aware review state, fixes actionable findings, pushes and verifies the new head, resolves evidenced threads through the orchestration policy, and merges only after gates pass.
expectations: The agent uses github:gh-address-comments for actionable review-thread work.
The agent classifies comments and preserves ambiguous or human-owned decisions.
The agent refreshes PR state after pushing fixes.
The agent respects external review budget rules before requesting another review cycle.

evals.prompt: Get this merged, but check the repo-local instructions first; this repo may require maintainer approval before resolving threads or merging.
evals.expected_output: The agent still uses the merge-closeout wrapper, discovers local policy before acting, follows any autonomy limits, and reports owner-controlled gates instead of forcing full autonomous closeout.
expectations: The agent treats local policy discovery as an early step, not a final afterthought.
The agent applies repo-local guidance to ready-for-review, thread resolution, merge method, branch cleanup, and deploy closeout.
The agent does not resolve threads or merge when local policy reserves those actions for another owner.
The agent reports the exact owner-controlled gate when full autonomy is not allowed.

evals.prompt: Please just write a better PR description for this pull request.
evals.expected_output: The wrapper should not be used as the main workflow; the agent should use the PR-description skill and avoid merge-oriented actions.
expectations: The agent does not mark ready, address comments, request review, or merge.
The agent narrows the task to PR body work.
The agent avoids implying merge readiness unless the user asks for closeout.

evals.prompt: Can you review PR #17 and tell me what you think?
evals.expected_output: The wrapper should not turn a review request into a merge attempt; the agent should take a code-review stance and report findings.
expectations: The agent does not publish, mark ready, resolve threads, or merge.
The agent treats the request as review-only unless the user adds a merge goal.
The agent reports findings before summary.
